name: The Tipton Latham Bank, National Association
description_short: Independent community bank serving Tipton and Latham, Missouri since 1906, offering checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and personal/business loans with FDIC insurance.
description_long:
The Tipton Latham Bank, National Association has operated as a locally-owned and independently-run community bank for over 115 years, establishing deep roots in the Tipton and Latham, Missouri communities. The bank maintains two physical branch locations and emphasizes personalized service typical of regional financial institutions.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. On the deposit side, they provide checking accounts, savings accounts with no minimum opening balance, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 3 to 60 months. Savings products include traditional savings, Gold and Platinum tiered accounts, and IRAs for retirement planning. Lending services encompass business loans for commercial expansion, personal loans for debt consolidation and emergencies, and debit cards for everyday transactions. The bank is FDIC-insured, providing deposit protection backed by the U.S. Government.

The Tipton Latham Bank distinguishes itself through its independent community focus rather than operating as a large regional or national chain. The institution offers competitive deposit rates—6-month CDs at 4.00% APY and money market accounts ranging from 1.85% to 2.50% APY depending on balance tier. Service fees are transparent and clearly disclosed, with no ATM fees at their own terminals. They provide online banking, check reordering, and financial calculators to support customer decision-making.

The primary limitation is geographic accessibility—with only two locations in Tipton and Latham, Missouri, the bank serves a limited footprint and may be inconvenient for non-local customers. Savings account rates (0.35% APY) are relatively modest compared to online-only banks. The company's small-bank model may mean fewer advanced products or services available compared to larger institutions, though this trade-off is common in community banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts with no minimum opening deposit
Money market accounts with tiered interest rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with 3 to 60-month terms
Business loans for commercial expansion and growth
Personal loans for debt consolidation and emergencies
Financial calculators for rate and payment calculations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
No minimum opening deposit required for savings accounts
Competitive CD rates up to 4.00% APY for 6-month terms as of March 2026
Tiered money market accounts with APY up to 2.50% for $500,000+ balances
No fees for ATM usage at their own terminals
Community-focused independent bank operating since 1906 with local decision-making
Transparent fee structure clearly disclosed on rates page
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two branches in Tipton and Latham, Missouri, restricting accessibility for non-local customers
Savings account rate of 0.35% APY is substantially lower than online-only banks and money market alternatives
Minimum opening deposits required for higher-yield products ($750 for Gold Account, $2,500 for Platinum and Money Market)
Limited product diversity compared to regional or national banks, may lack advanced services
best_for: Residents of Tipton and Latham, Missouri seeking personalized community banking relationships
Savers interested in CDs and money market accounts with competitive rates on larger balances
Small business owners and entrepreneurs needing local commercial lending relationships
diagnosis: Tipton Latham Bank is best suited for residents of Tipton and Latham, Missouri who value personal relationships with local banking staff and are willing to accept geographic limitations in exchange for community-focused service. The main caveat is that non-local customers or those seeking 24/7 digital-only banking experiences should consider alternatives, as this institution operates exclusively through two physical branches in a small service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
